title: RNA extraction from Aiptasia larvae using Zymo Quick-DNA/RNA Microprep Plus Kit
author: Christian CR Renicke
materials: Zymo Research Quick-DNA/RNA Microprep Plus Kit

steps.title: Transfer ~200 larvae in 40 µL ASW into a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer ~200 larvae in 40 µL ASW into a 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tube. Use a 10-µL or a 20-µL pipet which uses 10-µL XL tips, e.g., Eppendorf Research Plus 2 – 20 µL, light gray, 3123000098. 20 µl should allow to take up 20-30 larvae with practice, to concentrate them further do a series of pipetting steps into smaller and smaller drops of ASW on a new polystyrene surface (e.g., lid of a 60-mm disposable Petri Dish).

steps.title: Thaw the samples at RT and...
steps.instruction: Thaw the samples at RT and wait until they have reached RT themselves. Vortex thoroughly.

steps.title: Finish taking all samples, then store...
steps.instruction: Finish taking all samples, then store them at −80°C until extractions. This assumes that further samples need to be taken at other days. I only taking samples for a single time point, keep them at RT and proceed with the RNA extraction.

steps.title: Add 120 µL 1× DNA/RNA Shield and vortex briefly
steps.instruction: Add 120 µL 1× DNA/RNA Shield and vortex briefly.

steps.title: OPTIONAL
steps.instruction: OPTIONAL: Add 8 µL Proteinase K and 16 µL PK Digestion Buffer to the 160-µL samples. In our experience, this increases the RNA yield. If processing multiple samples make a master mix.

steps.title: Add 368 µL 100% ethanol to...
steps.instruction: Add 368 µL 100% ethanol to the now 368-µL sample and mix well.

steps.title: Spin at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT
steps.instruction: Spin at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT. SAVE FLOW-THROUGH! (That’s where the RNA is!)

steps.title: OPTIONAL
steps.instruction: OPTIONAL: Transfer the sample into a Zymo-Spin IC-XM Column (yellow) sitting in a 1.5-mL nuclease-free tube. This is only if you are using the Quick-DNA/RNA Microprep Plus Kit and is not required but makes life easier on the DNase I step later on.

steps.title: Add 184 DNA/RNA Lysis Buffer to...
steps.instruction: Add 184 DNA/RNA Lysis Buffer to the samples (equal to sample volume), mix well.

steps.title: OPTIONAL
steps.instruction: OPTIONAL: Mix well and incubate at 25°C for 30 min.

steps.title: Transfer sample to Zymo-Spin IC Column...
steps.instruction: Transfer sample to Zymo-Spin IC Column (clear) sitting in a 2-mL collection tube.

steps.title: Spin at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT
steps.instruction: Spin at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT. Discard flow-through.

steps.title: Add 400 µL DNA/RNA Wash Buffer to the column
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL DNA/RNA Wash Buffer to the column. Spin at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT. Discard flow-through.

steps.title: Add 40 µL of the DNase...
steps.instruction: Add 40 µL of the DNase I reaction mix (see "Before start" instructions) directly to the column matrix and incubate for 30 min at 25°C.

steps.title: Add 400 µL DNA/RNA Prep Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL DNA/RNA Prep Buffer to the column, spin at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT and discard flow-through.

steps.title: Add 700 µL DNA/RNA Wash Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 700 µL DNA/RNA Wash Buffer to the column, spin at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT and discard flow-through.

steps.title: Add 400 µL DNA/RNA Wash Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL DNA/RNA Wash Buffer to the column, spin at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT and discard flow-through.

steps.title: Carefully transfer the dry column into a new nuclease-free 1
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer the dry column into a new nuclease-free 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add 15 µL 65°C-warm nuclease-free water...
steps.instruction: Add 15 µL 65°C-warm nuclease-free water directly to the column and spin at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT.

steps.title: Add the eluate onto the column...
steps.instruction: Add the eluate onto the column again and spin at at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT.

steps.title: Transfer the eluate into a fresh nuclease-free 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the eluate into a fresh nuclease-free 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tube. Take aliquot for quality control if desired and store remaining RNA at −80°C or proceed with downstream application.
